title: Ageless Linux – Software for humans of indeterminate age
author: nateb2022
aiHeadline: Linux finally embraces the idea that maybe you don't want to die, but also don't want to update your OS every five minutes.

title: What happens when US economic data becomes unreliable
author: inaros
aiHeadline: Because nothing says 'trust us' like making your economic data so unreliable that even your own economists can't tell if we're in a recession or just really bad at math.

title: RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one
author: edward
aiHeadline: Because nothing says 'we care about your performance' like selling you a RAM kit with one real stick and one fake one, just to give you that illusion of speed you've always deserved.

title: Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025)
author: bilsbie
aiHeadline: Montana proves once again that when it comes to tech legislation, they're either way ahead of the curve or just really into the idea of 'right to compute' in a way that makes everyone else question their priorities.

title: XML is a cheap DSL
author: y1n0
aiHeadline: Because nothing says 'efficient and modern' like using XML, the programming equivalent of duct tape and paper clips, to create your domain-specific language.
